Class MacOsBleScanner

java.lang.Object
ch.varani.lego.ble.impl.macos.MacOsBleScanner
All Implemented Interfaces:
BleScanner, AutoCloseable
public final class MacOsBleScanner extends Object implements BleScanner
macOS implementation of BleScanner backed by CoreBluetooth via JNI.

On construction the class loads libble-macos.dylib from the JAR (extracted to a temp file by NativeLibraryLoader) and calls NativeBridge.init() to allocate the underlying CBCentralManager.

All blocking native calls are dispatched on a cached thread-pool so that the CompletableFuture returned to callers never blocks the JVM thread that constructed the future.

The native side calls back into onDeviceFound(String, String, int) for every discovered peripheral. CoreBluetooth delivers those callbacks on its own internal dispatch queue, not on a JVM thread, so the JNI bridge attaches that thread to the JVM before making the call.

Thread safety: all public methods are thread-safe.

Since:
1.0

  • Constructor Details

    • MacOsBleScanner

      public MacOsBleScanner() throws BleException
      Constructs a MacOsBleScanner by loading the native library and initialising the CoreBluetooth central manager.
      Throws:
      BleException - if the native library cannot be loaded or the Bluetooth adapter does not become ready in time

  • Method Details

    • startScan

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> startScan(@NonNull ScanCallback callback)
      Starts a passive BLE scan and delivers discovered peripherals to the given callback.

      The returned future completes when the platform has confirmed that scanning has started, or completes exceptionally with a BleException if scanning cannot be started (e.g. Bluetooth is disabled or permission was denied).

      If a scan is already running, calling this method again stops the previous scan and starts a fresh one with the new callback.

      Equivalent to startScan(String, ScanCallback) with a null service UUID filter (discovers all nearby peripherals).

      Specified by:
      startScan in interface BleScanner
      Parameters:
      callback - invoked for each discovered peripheral; must not be null
      Returns:
      a future that completes when scanning has started

    • startScan

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> startScan(@Nullable String serviceUuid, @NonNull ScanCallback callback)
      Starts a filtered BLE scan that only reports peripherals advertising the specified GATT service UUID.

      Calls NativeBridge.startScan(long, String) on the worker executor. Any previously registered scan is implicitly replaced.

      Specified by:
      startScan in interface BleScanner
      Parameters:
      serviceUuid - the 128-bit UUID string to filter on; null means no filter
      callback - invoked for each matching peripheral; must not be null
      Returns:
      a future that completes when scanning has started

    • stopScan

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> stopScan()
      Stops the active scan.

      If no scan is running this method is a no-op. The returned future completes when the platform has confirmed that scanning has stopped.

      Specified by:
      stopScan in interface BleScanner
      Returns:
      a future that completes when scanning has stopped

    • isScanning

      public boolean isScanning()
      Returns true if a scan is currently running.
      Specified by:
      isScanning in interface BleScanner
      Returns:
      true while a scan is active

    • close

      public void close() throws BleException
      Stops any active scan and releases all platform resources held by this scanner.

      Stops any active scan, calls NativeBridge.destroy(long), and releases all CoreBluetooth resources.

      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface BleScanner
      Throws:
      BleException - if releasing resources fails
